today.title: Mike / Gab Weekly Sync
today.synopsis: Weekly 1-2-1 with Gabriel (Senior Director, Product Development) via Zoom. First meeting of the day — lots to debrief after a busy Thursday: candidate interview, Cosmo Spins Instagram/rotation resolution, Sprint 9 regression completion, and IUJ engagement data shared with Colin.
discussion_topics: WC Predictor sprint sequencing — design (GDCU-8935 Miguel, In Progress), DB/API (GDCU-8995 Kelvin), admin panel (GDCU-8996 HIGH, unassigned — who's taking it?)
Cosmo Spins rotation block (GDCU-9007) — Gabriel's Thursday conclusion: Instagram doesn't allow landscape so removing the restriction is correct; Novica to research game optimisation
IUJ engagement data — only 30/12,000 unique users have levelled up; Colin briefed last night; discuss product mechanic options (points threshold, UI discoverability)
Candidate interview debrief — Thursday interview, Gabriel felt candidate not hands-on and relies on others; Mike's view: smart but not advanced enough; agree next steps
Vic/data gap plan — Colin says don't rely on Vic (at risk of redundancy); agree David utilisation plan and knowledge-transfer priorities
today.prep_notes: Review Thursday's group DM re Cosmo Spins Instagram limitations before the call. Check GDCU-8996 (WC admin panel, HIGH, unassigned) — raise owner question with Gabriel. IUJ analysis already sent to Colin last night so Gabriel may not have that context yet. Zoom: https://gdcgroup.zoom.us/j/81319832012

today.title: Project (working block)
today.synopsis: Mike's own calendar block titled 'Project' — no attendees listed, likely protected focus time. Good window to work through In Product reviews (GDCU-9007, GDCU-8971, GDCU-8902) and the LinkedIn/HR question before the afternoon standup.
today.prep_notes: Use this window: review and action all 3 In Product tickets, reply to Jess re LinkedIn rec (HR check first), and prep standup talking points on WC Predictor ownership gap (GDCU-8996).

today.title: GDCU Assemble 💥 Standup
today.synopsis: Full-team Friday standup. Sprint 9 regression completed yesterday — sprint is effectively wrapped. Three In Product tickets await Mike's sign-off. A new nav bug (GDCU-9006) was raised yesterday. WC Predictor admin panel (GDCU-8996, HIGH) has no owner — Friday standup is the right place to surface this.
discussion_topics: Sprint 9 wrap — regression passed (14/05), automation done; sprint formally closed by Chloe this morning
In Product sign-offs outstanding: GDCU-9007 (Cosmo rotation block, Harden), GDCU-8971 (Cosmo Spins how-to videos, Novica), GDCU-8902 (Sports Quiz design system, Jessica)
GDCU-9006 new bug — Search icon missing on scroll (filed by Shane 14/05, Backlog, unassigned); assign and prioritise for Sprint 10
WC Predictor ownership gap — GDCU-8996 (admin panel, HIGH) unassigned; GDCU-8995 (DB/API, HIGH) with Kelvin; GDCU-8935 (design) with Miguel
GDCU-8810 developer accounts still Blocked — Day 9; RotoWire contact needed to shortcut timeline
today.prep_notes: Vic Dadson is listed as an attendee — she returned this week but Colin has cautioned not to rely on her. Have In Product decisions ready before this meeting. Know who can take GDCU-8996 (admin panel). Zoom: https://gdcgroup.zoom.us/j/4789482034

action_items.action: REVIEW 3 IN PRODUCT TICKETS — All need Mike's sign-off today (Friday): (1) GDCU-9007 Remove Cosmo Spins Rotation Block (Harden, updated 14/05 21:29 — last night). Removes landscape-forced mode from Cosmo Spins so social ad users can play in portrait; Gabriel confirmed Instagram can't force landscape anyway. (2) GDCU-8971 Create How-to Videos for Cosmo Spins Reskin (Novica, updated 14/05 11:11). Knowledge-sharing videos for the team on the reskin process. (3) GDCU-8902 Sports Quiz as Design System Pressure Test (Jessica, updated 15/05 08:20 — this morning). UX audit of Sports Quiz prototype at hungry-jepsen.vercel.app; output is Claude Code prompts for codebase iteration. Review and approve or provide feedback on all three before the afternoon standup.

WC PREDICTOR ADMIN PANEL — GDCU-8996 is HIGH priority, unassigned, and sitting in Backlog. The DB/API (GDCU-8995, Kelvin) and design (GDCU-8935, Miguel) both have owners but the admin panel build has nobody. This needs an owner assigned before or at today's standup. Hard deadline is 11 June — the admin panel is a prerequisite for content entry.

JESSICA LINKEDIN RECOMMENDATION — Jessica asked Mike for a LinkedIn recommendation yesterday (14/05 11:35). Mike responded: 'I don't have a problem but I need to check with HR first Jess.' Jessica is in a redundancy consultation process. Check with HR today on the policy for writing recommendations during a live consultation period, then respond to Jess.

VIC REDUNDANCY — DATA GAP PLAN URGENT. Vic is back but at risk of redundancy. Colin told Mike last night: 'I wouldn't rely on getting much from Vic between now and the next few weeks fwiw. Safer to utilise David as much as we can.' Mike's data gap analysis sent to Colin on 14/05 lays out the problem (tracking patchy, Bloomreach not wired in, no push reporting, data in silos). Immediate actions: (1) Align with Vic on a structured knowledge-transfer/brain-dump plan before she leaves. (2) Agree with David Mulero which requirements to prioritise alongside BI. (3) Confirm Tableau access timeline with BI. (4) Monitor IUJ tickets (GDCU-8958 started today) to see if they move the engagement needle.

GDCU-8810 DEVELOPER ACCOUNTS — Still Blocked (Harden, In Progress). Day 9 since 06/05. Apple/Google org-level accounts are a prerequisite for app submission. Primary shortcut: contact RotoWire team to check if GDC Group org accounts already exist (could reduce 6-8 weeks to 1-2 weeks). Escalate today if not already done — GDCU-8969 (create app in App Store Connect, Carlos) is next in the pipeline.

GDCU-9006 NEW BUG — Search icon is not appearing in the nav bar when the page is scrolled (filed by Shane 14/05, Backlog, unassigned). QA verified all go-live tickets yesterday and Robin signed off, but this nav regression needs to be assessed and assigned. Check if it's linked to the Ace Nav redesign (GDCU-8936, which went live recently). Assign to appropriate dev and set sprint priority.

GDCU-8432 GEO LOCATE IMAGES — Still Blocked (To Do, unassigned). Geo-located images on the Global HP have been blocked for some time with no owner. Review whether this is still a priority and either assign for Sprint 10 or formally de-scope.

IUJ ENGAGEMENT — PRODUCT MECHANIC REVIEW. Data (from David) shows only 30 out of 12,000 unique IUJ users have levelled up. Tournaments dominate (42k plays, 70% of activity), but the progression loop isn't closing. Mike has tickets in place: GDCU-8958 (IUJ Banners, Harden, In Progress — started today) and GDCU-8795 (Refer-a-Friend CTA update, Novica, In QA). Monitor whether these address the discoverability problem. If they don't move the needle, consider removing the feature (Mike flagged this option to Colin last night).

GDCU-8972 CLAUDE DESIGN IMPROVEMENTS — Mike confirmed with Chloe yesterday (14/05 12:04): this ticket doesn't need sprint planning inclusion, 2 SPs tops, Ciara to update design system based on stakeholder feedback. GDCU-8972 is in Backlog with no assignee. Assign to Ciara, set story points to 2, and confirm she's clear on scope (update design system based on Claude Design feedback — Mike gave Ciara feedback on spacing vs images yesterday).

CANDIDATE DEBRIEF — Mike and Gabriel interviewed a candidate yesterday (14/05). Gabriel: not hands-on, relies on others, exhausted by the interview. Mike: smart but not advanced enough for what we need; could be a junior candidate. Agree with Gabriel on next steps — decline, hold, or explore a junior-level path.

needs_input.context: NEW — moved In Product yesterday evening (14/05 21:29). Removes the rotation block that prevented social ad users from playing Cosmo Spins in portrait mode on mobile. Gabriel confirmed in a group chat yesterday that Instagram doesn't support landscape mode anyway, so removing the restriction is the correct call. Mike needs to review and approve. Stakeholders: Gabriel Cornoiu, Mike Pett, Joshua Kerr, Dean Ryan, Eoin McCarthy, Novica Vukobratovic, Ciara Carroll.

Novica has created videos documenting how to reskin the Cosmo Spins game and banners, plus Confluence docs, to enable other team members to complete the task independently. Mike to review the output (videos + docs) and approve. Stakeholders: Eoin McCarthy, Novica Vukobratovic, Joshua Smith, Gabriel Cornoiu, Kelvin Yee.

needs_input.summary: [GDC][UX] - Use Sports Quiz as a Pressure Test for the GDC Design System
needs_input.context: Updated this morning (15/05 08:20). Jessica has completed a UX audit of the Sports Quiz Vercel prototype (hungry-jepsen.vercel.app) against the GDC design system. Output is a set of Claude Code prompts for direct codebase iteration — covering visual hierarchy, typography, spacing, component affordance, and empty states. Mike to review the prompts and approve for dev handoff. Stakeholders: Chloe Christie, Gabriel Cornoiu, Joshua Smith.

blocked.note: Day 9 blocked (since 06/05). First action: contact RotoWire team to check if GDC Group org accounts already exist — could shortcut 6-8 weeks to 1-2 weeks. Next in pipeline: GDCU-8969 (create app in App Store Connect, Carlos). Mobile app submission pipeline is fully gated on this.

needs_input.note: NEW — moved In Product 14/05 11:11. Novica's knowledge-sharing videos for Cosmo Spins reskin process. Mike to review videos and documentation.

needs_input.note: Updated today 15/05 08:20. Jessica's design system audit of the Sports Quiz prototype — outputs are Claude Code prompts for codebase iteration. Mike to review and approve.

UK sitewide QA audit of the bottom nav bar complete. Robin verified all tickets that went live on 14/05 — all clear.

Sprint 9 regression testing passed. Sprint 9 is fully wrapped. ✅

Gabriel confirmed Cosmo Spins Instagram limitation in group DM (landscape not supported so rotation removal is correct).
